# Data — Project 1: CAT Risk Modeling

## Dataset: Synthetic Natural Disaster Loss Dataset

**Generated by:** `notebooks/cat_risk_modeling.ipynb` (Step 2)  
**File created:** `disaster_data.csv` (auto-generated on first run)  
**Size:** 900 rows × 5 columns (20 countries × 45 years)

## Why Synthetic Data?

The dataset is built using real historical anchors from published sources:
- EM-DAT International Disaster Database (CRED)
- Munich Re NatCatSERVICE records
- Swiss Re Sigma disaster reports

Real events embedded in the model:

| Event | Year | Country | Deaths | Damage |
|-------|------|---------|--------|--------|
| Bhola Cyclone | 1970 | Bangladesh | ~300,000 | $50B |
| Tangshan Earthquake | 1976 | China | ~242,000 | $40B |
| Great Famine | 1983 | Ethiopia | ~400,000 | $5B |
| Hurricane Katrina | 2005 | USA | ~1,800 | $200B |
| Kashmir Earthquake | 2005 | Pakistan | ~73,000 | $15B |
| Sichuan Earthquake | 2008 | China | ~87,000 | $150B |
| Haiti Earthquake | 2010 | Haiti | ~220,000 | $14B |
| Tohoku Tsunami | 2011 | Japan | ~19,000 | $235B |
| Typhoon Haiyan | 2013 | Philippines | ~6,300 | $13B |

## Column Descriptions

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `country_code` | string | 3-letter ISO country code |
| `country_name` | string | Full country name |
| `year` | integer | Calendar year (1970–2014) |
| `deaths` | integer | Total deaths from disasters that year |
| `economic_damage_usd` | float | Economic damage in USD |

## Data Generation Methodology

Three scenario types per country-year:
1. **Known catastrophe** — real historical figures ± 10% random variation
2. **Random catastrophic year** — 5% probability (1-in-20 year event)
3. **Normal year** — Log-Normal distribution (mirrors real actuarial severity modeling)
